item.title: Weeds
item.description:
The apartment we rent has a terrace. The part of the terrace that isn't wood decking or planters is covered in gravel. Despite a lack of soil, we're continually battling weeds that sprout up from the gravel. Fortunately they're easy to pull because there's not much for the roots to cling to.

This Spring though we were happy to discover that many of the "weeds" are actually arugula! We grew arugula in our raised garden beds last year and let it go to seed, and this is the result. Last year we found oregano growing wild amongst the hedges in the planters.

It makes me smile to think that future tenants of this apartment will have to contend with arugula, basil, chives, kale, mint, parsley, rosemary, sage, spinach, and thyme sprouting up everywhere. 😃

item.title: Exercise Streak Day 1 ... Again
item.description:
Today I restarted a daily exercise habit once again. For me that means at least a 30-minute walk or taking a watercraft out on the lake. Regular exercise is good for me and makes me feel good. Being out in nature works wonders for my mind and soul (and lets my subconscious ponder complicated coding issues). Yet I continually struggle to establish and keep a routine.

Here's a cow pasture I can reach in less than five minutes' walk from my apartment. I love living next to farm land. There are chickens, cows, horses, and even some wallabies I can visit (not to mention the usual swarm of farm cats). There's little traffic and plenty of beautiful vistas to enjoy. So why is it so hard to make myself get out here regularly?

I'm posting this as a reminder to myself to get off my ass and get outside. It's always worth it.

item.title: Fear
item.description:
Apparently I am afraid of mail. At least official-looking mail from government agencies.

Just now when I returned from the store, there were three letters from the canton addressed to each of us. I just received my settlement permit, but apparently I'm still nervous about my status here in Switzerland. Various thoughts ran through my head: "What if they're kicking us out?", "What if I've unwittingly broken some rule and they're rescinding my settlement permit?", etc.

It turns out that the subject of the letters was entirely benign: we are no longer subject to mandatory tax withholding at source. But my reaction, that gut fear of having messed up, tells me that I still have a lot of work to do on my integration.

(I have made progress on this in the past year. Letters addressed solely to me no longer trigger this reaction, but in this case the identical letters to all three of us was something I don't think had every happened before and seemed somehow ominous. I was correct however in my assumption that this was related to my recent change in status.)

item.title: Website as Repo
item.description:
When I decided that I wanted to ditch WordPress and rewrite my website as a static site, I was unsure where and how I wanted to host it. I've paid Bluehost to host my old WordPress site, but I wasn't quite ready to nuke my existing WordPress install and replace it (and I might never be), so I needed another domain and another hosting solution.

The domain part was easy because I had a domain[1] that was parked, but because this whole static HTML thing is very much an experiment (one that I am enjoying thus far), I didn't want to spend more money on hosting just yet. Besides, for static HTML sites there are numerous options for free hosting.

The simplest solution for me was to just host it through GitHub Pages. I have already been hosting my CV there since 2018. This means that my entire website is a public repo, with all of its files and commit history fully visible to the public. I'm OK with that though; websites are public by design.

I wanted source control for my new website anyway. That way I can review file history and easily revert anything that borks the site. Having the source control be the website just kills two birds with one stone. So far I am pleased with the arrangement.

  1. Crazy Milk Software is my LLC for consulting/freelancing, and so several years back I had acquired the crazymilk.net domain. ↩

item.title: Green Classic Space Minifig Math
item.description:
LEGO released the first recolor of their Classic Space minifigure in 2014. The LEGO Ideas Exo Suit set contained two Classic Space minifigs in green, but they had modern visored motorcycle helmets and more detailed heads.

Since then LEGO has issued recolors of Classic Space torsos (if not entire minifigures) in gray, pink, orange, purple, and brown, but it wasn't until 2019 that LEGO created a modern version of the original Classic Space helmet (initially in white, pink, and yellow) for the Benny's Space Squad set.

Ten years after the Exo Suit, LEGO finally issued a green recolor of this helmet in the Monkie Kid Megapolis City set. I wasn't going to buy four copies of this large set, but I did order four helmets from BrickLink. Now I can finally evolve my four green Exo Suit minifigs to their final form.

Last Fall I did something similar to build purple Classic Space minifigs.

item.title: Settling in
item.description:
Yesterday I received my settlement permit (permanent residency / C Permit) for Switzerland. That was one of my goals for this year, and now it's handled.

Permanent residency has numerous advantages, such as the ability to move between cantons or change jobs at will. It is valid for 5 years instead of 1 or 2, and the renewal process is much simpler than for a residence permit (B Permit). Before I need to renew it, I will be eligible to apply for Swiss citizenship should we still be here and should I desire it.

Does this mean I am fully integrated? By no means, but it is a step along the way. It's hard for an introvert to integrate anywhere, and Switzerland may be more challenging in this regard than many places.

On a related note, after six years and eight months living in Switzerland, I have finally been invited to a Swiss person's house. It's to play D&D, so even better!

item.title: Ägeri Farbig
item.description:
Ägeri Farbig means something like "Colored Ägeri" or "Vivid Ägeri". Ägeri is the name of the little pre-alpine valley (and its lake) where I live.

Ägeri Farbig is a volunteer organization of knitters. Each year they decorate something outdoors with colorful yarn squares. In previous years they have wrapped trees in local parks. This year they decorated this old wooden house in the middle of Unterägeri village. The house is decorated with over 7,000 knitted squares contributed by nearly 300 knitters from here and abroad.

It's on the route I take to walk to a couple bakeries and the lake, so I see it often. Whenever I do it always brightens my day.

item.title: Bärenhunger auf Bärlauch?
item.description:
Are you hungry like a bear for wild garlic?

Why, yes. Yes I am!

Bärlauch, known in English as wild garlic or ramsons, is something I only discovered upon moving to Switzerland. It grows wild in the forests in springtime. Supposedly bears like it (not that there are many wild bears here to eat it).

Every Spring I look forward to the few weeks when fresh bärlauch is available in the local grocery stores. I use it in pestos, pizzas, quiches, pastas, etc. In a world where many produce items are available year-round (potentially at great environmental cost), I appreciate the local nature and temporal scarcity of bärlauch.

item.title: First SUP of 2024
item.description:
Today felt like an early summer day, except it's mid-April. I should be worried about whether we'll get one last snow and not beach weather. In any case it was a gorgeous morning to be out on Lake Ägeri. Apart from a couple fishing boats and some waterfowl, I had the lake to myself.

There's a bakery located by the boat ramp where I put in, so I often grab a cup of coffee and balance it on the board. After a 30-minute paddle, I was sitting on my board and enjoying my first coffee of the day when I took this photo.

item.title: Trying my Hand at a Static Website
item.description:
I've been using WordPress to run my blog since I started it in 2011. Initially I used WordPress dot com, but in 2018 I switched over to hosting a WordPress dot org site. Lately though I've gotten tired of all WordPress' cruft and have been longing for a much simpler static site.

Inspired by Louie Mantia Jr's How to Make a Damn Website post, here is my first post to the new site. I'm currently using GitHub Pages to host it because it's free and I'm not ready to attempt to port over 13 years of posts from my WordPress blog just yet.

Who knows? Maybe I'll even learn some HTML and CSS along the way. Fingers crossed.
